Tests the behavior of creating an object from an undefined value in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that demonstrates the behavior of the `Object` constructor when it is called with `undefined` as an argument. It declares a variable `a` and assigns it the result of `Object(undefined)`, which creates an empty object. The script then uses an `assert` statement to verify that the type of `a` is `'object'`. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific behavior of the JavaScript `Object` constructor.
